title: Listening - Part 3: Cooking at Home
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they have changed the way they cook at home. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for the change. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they have changed the way they cook at home. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for the change. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about changes to their cooking habits.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I used to come home tired and put a frozen meal in the oven nearly every evening. It was easy, but during a health check the nurse asked about my diet and pointed out how much salt those meals can contain. [19]I now cook simple dishes using vegetables, beans and fresh ingredients because I want my everyday meals to be better for my health.[/19] It takes more effort, although I prepare several portions at once, and I’ve discovered that fresh food tastes much better too.||Speaker 2 Man: I was always throwing away half bags of salad, vegetables at the back of the fridge and bread that had gone hard. I hated seeing it in the bin, especially when food prices were rising. [20]Now I plan meals around ingredients that need using first and turn leftovers into soups or lunches, because I want far less of the food I buy to be wasted.[/20] I’m saving some money as well, but the main satisfaction is that almost everything gets eaten.||Speaker 3 Woman: My two flatmates and I used to prepare separate meals and eat at different times, usually looking at our phones. We got on well but hardly saw one another properly. [21]We decided that every Tuesday we would choose a recipe, shop together and cook one meal as a group, mainly because it gives us an enjoyable evening in each other's company.[/21] Some attempts have been more successful than others, but even the burnt pizza made us laugh for a long time.||Speaker 4 Man: My grandfather used to cook for everyone at family celebrations, but since he became less mobile he has missed being able to prepare his favourite dishes. He gave me his handwritten recipes last year. [22]I began practising them because I wanted to serve the meals he loves when he comes to our house and show him that his family traditions will continue.[/22] The first pie wasn't quite right, but he smiled throughout the meal and offered plenty of helpful advice.||Speaker 5 Woman: After travelling in Thailand, I kept talking about the dishes I’d tasted there. The restaurants near my home only serve a few familiar options, and they didn’t remind me of the meals I’d enjoyed abroad. [23]I signed up for online cooking lessons with a Thai chef because I wanted to understand the ingredients and techniques behind food from a culture I’d become interested in.[/23] My kitchen now contains spices I'd never previously heard of, and preparing the dishes brings back great memories.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I used to come home tired and put a frozen meal in the oven nearly every evening. It was easy, but during a health check the nurse asked about my diet and pointed out how much salt those meals can contain. I now cook simple dishes using vegetables, beans and fresh ingredients because I want my everyday meals to be better for my health. It takes more effort, although I prepare several portions at once, and I’ve discovered that fresh food tastes much better too.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: I was always throwing away half bags of salad, vegetables at the back of the fridge and bread that had gone hard. I hated seeing it in the bin, especially when food prices were rising. Now I plan meals around ingredients that need using first and turn leftovers into soups or lunches, because I want far less of the food I buy to be wasted. I’m saving some money as well, but the main satisfaction is that almost everything gets eaten.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: My two flatmates and I used to prepare separate meals and eat at different times, usually looking at our phones. We got on well but hardly saw one another properly. We decided that every Tuesday we would choose a recipe, shop together and cook one meal as a group, mainly because it gives us an enjoyable evening in each other's company. Some attempts have been more successful than others, but even the burnt pizza made us laugh for a long time.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: My grandfather used to cook for everyone at family celebrations, but since he became less mobile he has missed being able to prepare his favourite dishes. He gave me his handwritten recipes last year. I began practising them because I wanted to serve the meals he loves when he comes to our house and show him that his family traditions will continue. The first pie wasn't quite right, but he smiled throughout the meal and offered plenty of helpful advice.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: After travelling in Thailand, I kept talking about the dishes I’d tasted there. The restaurants near my home only serve a few familiar options, and they didn’t remind me of the meals I’d enjoyed abroad. I signed up for online cooking lessons with a Thai chef because I wanted to understand the ingredients and techniques behind food from a culture I’d become interested in. My kitchen now contains spices I'd never previously heard of, and preparing the dishes brings back great memories.

options.text: to spend enjoyable time with other people

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker started preparing fresh meals in order to improve her diet (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker reorganised meal planning chiefly in order to avoid throwing food away (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The flatmates' cooking routine was created as a social activity they could enjoy together (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker is learning recipes in order to make a grandfather happy and preserve his dishes (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker is learning Thai cookery as a way of exploring a culture experienced while travelling (evidence [23]).
